_meta.instrument: IPIP Big-Five Factor Markers (100-item) — 한국어 번역
_meta.full_name: International Personality Item Pool (IPIP) Big-Five Factor Markers, 100-item version (based on Goldberg's 1992 unipolar Big-Five markers) (Korean translation)
_meta.license: Public domain. Per the IPIP website (ipip.ori.org): "The items and scales are in the public domain, which means that one can copy, edit, translate, or use them for any purpose without asking permission and without paying a fee."
_meta.reuse_notes: 한국어 번역(파생물). 비상업 1단계용. LLM 번역이며 검증된 한국어 표준검사가 아님 — 실사용 전 재검수 권장. 라이선스는 원본을 따르되 번역은 파생물이므로 CC-SA/연구용 조건이 그대로 적용됨.
_meta.attribution: Items from the International Personality Item Pool (IPIP), based on Goldberg's Big-Five factor markers. Cite: Goldberg, L. R. (1992). The development of markers for the Big-Five factor structure. Psychological Assessment, 4, 26-42. And: Goldberg, L. R., Johnson, J. A., Eber, H. W., Hogan, R., Ashton, M. C., Cloninger, C. R., & Gough, H. G. (2006). The International Personality Item Pool and the future of public-domain personality measures. Journal of Research in Personality, 40, 84-96. Source of scales: International Personality Item Pool, https://ipip.ori.org/. · 한국어 번역: deepinme 번역 워크플로우(2026-07, LLM).
_meta.scoring_notes: Standard IPIP scoring: administer on a 5-point Likert scale (1 = Very Inaccurate ... 5 = Very Accurate); score + keyed items 1->5 and - keyed items reversed 5->1, then sum the 20 items within each factor. The 100-item version is composed of the 20-item scale for each of the five factors on the IPIP scoring-key page (reported alphas: Extraversion .91, Agreeableness .88, Conscientiousness .88, Emotional Stability .91, Intellect/Imagination .90). Keying breakdown by factor: Extraversion 10+/10-, Agreeableness 14+/6-, Conscientiousness 11+/9-, Emotional Stability 5+/15-, Intellect/Imagination 13+/7- (53 positive, 47 negative overall). Note: Factor IV is scored as Emotional Stability (the reverse pole of Neuroticism) and Factor V (Intellect/Imagination) corresponds to Openness. The separately-collected 50-item version (10 items per factor) is a proper subset of these 100 items.
_meta.cautions: All 100 items were fetched verbatim from the raw HTML of the official IPIP scoring-key page (https://ipip.ori.org/newBigFive5broadKey.htm); none were reconstructed from memory. The 100-item version equals the "20-item scale" for each of the five factors listed on that page; the same page also lists the "10-item scale" per factor, which is the 50-item version (a proper subset, collected elsewhere). Item text is preserved exactly including trailing periods; items are written as verb phrases with an implied first-person "I" stem (the questionnaire prepends "I..." at administration). Per-factor keying is unbalanced (e.g., Emotional Stability is only 5+/15-), which is faithful to the source, not an extraction error. The scoring key groups items by factor and does NOT specify a single canonical mixed administration order; the "num" values (1-100) are a sequential index I assigned in factor-grouped, +then- order for reference, not item numbers from the source. This instrument has no facet structure (it is a factor-marker set), so no facet labels are provided. Factor IV is scored toward Emotional Stability (reverse of Neuroticism) and Factor V (Intellect/Imagination) is the IPIP label for Openness. License verified as public domain from the IPIP homepage. | 번역본: 문항 순서·채점방향(keyed)·factor/facet은 원본과 동일. text_en에 원문 병기.

translation.method: LLM faithful translation (deepinme workflow, 2026-07) — 충실 번역이며 '쉬운말 리라이팅'은 별도 단계
translation.status: working draft — NOT a validated Korean instrument

qa.reviewer_overall: Very clean, high-quality translation set. All 100 source items are translated one-to-one with unique IDs 1-100 (count matches). Every item preserves the measured trait meaning and uses a consistent first-person self-report frame ('나는 …다'), and reverse-keyed items are correctly presented as plain affirmative statements rather than negated — the proper IPIP convention. Naturalness is high; the Korean reads idiomatically rather than as translationese. Only a handful of low-severity nits: item 85 adds an unwarranted 'enjoy' (즐겨) nuance, item 76 uses the stiff coinage '위협감', and item 59 is wordy/formal (착수); item 63 is a mild idiomatic shift. None of these change the construct being measured. Fidelity: high; naturalness: natural.

issues.problem: 'Use difficult words' is rendered as '어려운 단어를 즐겨 쓴다', where '즐겨' adds an enjoyment/preference nuance ('enjoy using') that is not in the neutral, habitual English source.
issues.suggestion: 나는 어려운 단어를 자주 쓴다. (or simply: 나는 어려운 단어를 쓴다.)

issues.problem: 'Feel threatened easily' → '쉽게 위협감을 느낀다'. '위협감' is a stiff/clinical coinage and reads slightly awkwardly.
'Find it difficult to get down to work' → '일에 집중해서 착수하는 것을 어려워한다' is wordy and '착수' is formal/번역투-ish for an everyday self-report item.
issues.suggestion: 나는 좀처럼 일을 시작하지 못한다. (or: 나는 일에 손을 대기가 어렵다.)

issues.problem: 'Am not easily bothered by things' → '웬만한 일로는 마음이 흔들리지 않는다' slightly shifts 'bothered/annoyed' toward 'shaken/swayed'; acceptable but not a literal match.
issues.suggestion: 나는 웬만한 일에는 별로 신경 쓰지 않는다. (only if a closer match to 'bothered' is desired; current version is defensible.)

verification.summary: 번역 QA(2차 에이전트): fidelity=high, naturalness=natural, count_match=True, issues=4

sources.title: IPIP Big-Five Factor Markers - Scoring Key (Factors I-V, 10- and 20-item scales)
International Personality Item Pool (IPIP) Home - public-domain statement
Administering IPIP Measures, with a 50-item Sample Questionnaire (scoring instructions)
Korean translation (deepinme workflow)
sources.license: translation is a derivative; see reuse_notes

items.text_en: Am the life of the party.

items.text: 나는 사람들과 함께 있을 때 편안함을 느낀다.
나는 파티에서 여러 다양한 사람들과 이야기를 나눈다.
items.text_en: Talk to a lot of different people at parties.
Don't mind being the center of attention.

items.text: 나는 사람들의 마음을 사로잡는 법을 안다.

items.text_en: Am skilled in handling social situations.

items.text: 나는 나서지 않고 뒤로 물러나 있는 편이다.
나는 나에게 관심이 쏠리는 것을 좋아하지 않는다.
items.text_en: Don't like to draw attention to myself.

items.text: 나는 낯선 사람들 앞에서는 조용한 편이다.
나는 다른 사람에게 먼저 다가가는 것을 어려워한다.
items.text_en: Find it difficult to approach others.

items.text: 나는 다른 사람들과 있을 때 자주 불편함을 느낀다.
나는 자신을 좀처럼 드러내지 않는 사람이다.
나는 다른 사람이 먼저 나서주기를 기다린다.
items.text_en: Wait for others to lead the way.

items.text: 나는 다른 사람을 위해 기꺼이 시간을 낸다.
나는 다른 사람의 감정을 함께 느낀다.
나는 사람들의 마음을 편하게 해 준다.
나는 다른 사람을 위로할 줄 안다.
나는 거의 모든 사람과 사이가 좋다.
items.text_en: Am on good terms with nearly everyone.
Have a good word for everyone.

items.text: 나는 다른 사람을 돕는 것을 좋아한다.
나는 다른 사람의 문제에는 관심이 없다.
items.text_en: Am not interested in other people's problems.

items.text: 나는 다른 사람을 별로 신경 쓰지 않는다.
나는 다른 사람에게 별로 관심이 없다.
items.text_en: Am not really interested in others.
Am hard to get to know.
Am indifferent to the feelings of others.

items.text: 나는 세부적인 것까지 꼼꼼히 신경 쓴다.
나는 해야 할 일을 바로바로 처리한다.

items.text_en: Do things according to a plan.

items.text: 나는 모든 것이 완벽해질 때까지 계속한다.

items.text_en: Make plans and stick to them.

items.text: 나는 질서와 규칙적인 것을 매우 좋아한다.
나는 물건을 제자리에 두는 것을 자주 잊는다.
items.text_en: Often forget to put things back in their proper place.

items.text: 나는 해야 할 일을 소홀히 한다.
나는 무슨 일이든 어중간하게 하다 만다.
items.text_en: Do things in a half-way manner.

items.text: 나는 일에 집중해서 착수하는 것을 어려워한다.
items.text_en: Find it difficult to get down to work.
Leave a mess in my room.
Am relaxed most of the time.

items.text: 나는 웬만한 일로는 마음이 흔들리지 않는다.
items.text_en: Am not easily bothered by things.
Get caught up in my problems.

items.text: 나는 여러 가지를 곰곰이 성찰하는 시간을 갖는다.
나는 대화를 더 깊이 있는 수준으로 끌어올린다.
items.text_en: Carry the conversation to a higher level.

items.text: 나는 많은 정보를 잘 다룰 수 있다.
items.text_en: Can handle a lot of information.

items.text: 나는 일을 하는 새로운 방식을 궁리해 내는 것을 좋아한다.
items.text_en: Love to think up new ways of doing things.

items.text: 나는 어렵고 도전적인 글을 읽는 것을 좋아한다.
나는 추상적인 개념을 이해하는 데 어려움을 느낀다.

items.text_en: Am not interested in abstract ideas.
Do not have a good imagination.

items.text: 나는 어떤 주제를 깊이 파고들지 않는다.
items.text_en: Will not probe deeply into a subject.
